quarc.help Sep 30 2015 CONTENTS QuaRC on EECS Instructional Computers QuaRC Licenses QuARC on EECS Instructional Computers ------------------------------------- QuaRC from Quanser (http://www.quanser.com) is a software suit

uanser (http://www.quanser.com) is a software suite for Windows that integrates with Simulink for rapid controls prototyping and hardware- in-the-loop testing. It generates soft real-time code, with performance parameters sufficient for use w

ith performance parameters sufficient for use with most of Quanser educational experiments. QuaRC is installed on 6 Windows PCs in 204 Cory for use by EE128, including 125-27, 125-30, 125-32, 125-33 QuaRC Licenses -------------- The licen

125-33 QuaRC Licenses -------------- The license server runs on workstation 125-32 in 125 Cory. If that is down, users on the other PCs get a "license expired" error. QuaRC licenses were originally obtained by Ferenc Kovac in 2008 and have

inally obtained by Ferenc Kovac in 2008 and have been renewed by Katherina Law. Quanser generously donated licenses in August 2014. EECS Instructional Support 378/384/386 Cory, 333 Soda inst@eecs.berkeley.edu
